items.title: Artemis II blasts ever closer to the far side of the Moon
items.summary: The mission's last, big push on its lunar journey takes humans out of the Earth's orbit for the first time since 1972.

items.title: Trump removes US Attorney General Pam Bondi
items.summary: Bondi's time as top US law enforcement officer was overshadowed by her handling of the Epstein files.

items.title: 'I haven't slept for days': Iranians describe mounting desperation after a month of war
items.summary: Ordinary people from various parts of Iran describe expanding strikes, economic pain and fear of repression.

items.title: Fears cost of water and beer to soar as India's scorching summer hits
items.summary: Makers of plastic and glass bottles are struggling to access raw materials as the war squeezes supplies.

items.title: 'Be serious... don't speak every day': Macron criticises Trump approach to Iran war
items.summary: The comments were an apparent jab at the US leader's sometimes contradictory stance on the US-Israeli war against Iran.

items.title: Judge dismisses most of Blake Lively's claims in Justin Baldoni harassment suit
items.summary: The judge left in place three allegations against Baldoni, meaning the civil trial will go ahead next month.

items.title: Brazil grants joint custody over pets in new law for separated couples
items.summary: Lawmakers in the Brazilian Congress view the law change as a reflection of pets' importance for their owners.

items.title: Hegseth asks US Army's top general to step down
items.summary: Gen Randy George was nominated for the role in 2023 by former President Joe Biden.

items.title: Iran's two largest steel plants shut down due to air strikes, companies say
items.summary: The strikes, which Iran says were first launched by Israel in co-ordination with the US last week, could cause major damage to the Iranian economy.

items.title: Ancient golden helmet recovered more than a year after Dutch heist
items.summary: The helmet and three golden bracelets were stolen in January 2025, but one bracelet is yet to be found.

items.title: Baby shot dead in pram in New York City, police say
items.summary: The seven-month-old child is the unintended victim of a suspected gang-related shooting, officials say.

items.title: Man dies in storm near Athens as Saharan dust shrouds Crete
items.summary: Several regions are flooded in mainland Greece, while a Saharan dust storm envelopes the island of Crete.

items.title: US lifts sanctions on Venezuelan interim leader Delcy Rodríguez
items.summary: It is the latest sign of warming relations between the US and Venezuela after Nicolás Maduro was seized in a commando raid.

items.title: Iranian Nobel laureate suffered suspected heart attack in prison, family says
items.summary: Narges Mohammadi's brother says he fears her life is in imminent danger and she needs to be transferred to a hospital.

items.title: Four toddlers stabbed to death at Ugandan nursery school
items.summary: The suspect is in custody but his motive is unknown, the police say in a brief statement.

items.title: Elon Musk's SpaceX set to be worth $1 trillion with planned public listing
items.summary: The company’s public stock debut is set to be one of the most valuable in history, and could make Musk the world's first trillionaire.

items.title: Magnitude 7.4 earthquake hits off Indonesia, killing one
items.summary: The quake struck between the islands of Manado and Ternate. Tsunami warnings have now been lifted.

items.title: Australia to crack down on gambling ads after years of criticism
items.summary: Australians lose more money to gambling, per capita, than anywhere else in the world.

items.title: Over 1,800 killed since junta seized power in Burkina Faso, rights group says
items.summary: Human Rights Watch says Capt Traoré, other military leaders and jihadists "may be liable" for the killings.

items.title: Utah teen identified as victim of serial killer Ted Bundy
items.summary: New DNA testing has solved the 51-year-old case of murdered teenager Laura Ann Aime, according to investigators.

items.title: US Supreme Court appears sceptical of Trump plan to limit birthright citizenship
items.summary: Trump attended the arguments in person, a rare move by a sitting president.

items.title: Alleged Bondi gunman loses court bid to suppress names of his family
items.summary: Lawyers for Naveed Akram had argued his mother, brother and sister live in "constant fear".

items.title: Arlo Parks: 'I got out of my head and into my body'
items.summary: The singer-songwriter found herself on the dancefloor. On a new album, she asks us to join her.

items.title: Myanmar's coup leader who set off a brutal civil war becomes president
items.summary: General Min Aung Hlaing has been chosen as the next president by the newly-elected parliament.

items.title: What nearly went wrong on Nasa's space mission - and what still could
items.summary: The Artemis II crew are now hurtling around the Earth's orbit, carrying out final tests and checks before they head towards the Moon.

items.title: Trump leaves key questions unanswered as he seeks to calm nerves over Iran war
items.summary: There were some glaring omissions in the US president's primetime address, writes the BBC's Gary O'Donoghue.

items.title: Iran's focus on survival means same regime still firmly in place
items.summary: Tehran is showing resilience despite Trump's claims of regime change, writes BBC Persian editor Amir Azimi.

items.title: What do Trump's latest comments on leaving Nato mean for the alliance?
items.summary: Trump has criticised Nato members for showing a lack of support for US objectives in Iran.

items.title: Peter Magyar, the former Orban ally vying for power in Hungary
items.summary: Magyar represents the biggest threat to Viktor Orban's rule in Hungary since he won the first of four consecutive victories in 2010.

items.title: What to know about Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche
items.summary: The former prosecutor has a long-standing relationship with President Trump, having represented him in the criminal hush-money case.

items.summary: The US is aiming to be the first country to land humans on the lunar surface again by 2028 - so what's this mission for?

items.summary: After delays and technical issues, the first crewed Moon mission in 50 years finally took off from Florida and is now in Earth's orbit.

items.title: Macron calls Trump's remarks on his marriage 'inelegant'
items.summary: French President Emmanuel Macron has said Donald Trump's comments about his marriage were "neither elegant nor up to standard".

items.title: Inside Nasa's moon spacesuit lab ahead of Artemis launch
items.summary: The Artemis II spacesuits are designed to protect the astronauts during launch and landing, as well as possible emergency scenarios.
